label: SIEM / Security Platform Engineer
description: Builds, operates, and scales SIEM and security data platform infrastructure — ingestion pipelines, log parsing, data modeling, and platform architecture rather than detection content development.

paths.name: Splunk Platform Track
paths.rationale: Splunk is the most deployed enterprise SIEM. The certification progression from Power User through Admin to Architect maps directly to platform engineering career levels.
pros: Splunk Admin and Architect certs required by name at most Splunk engineering roles
Strong employer recognition where Splunk is deployed
cons: Platform-specific — Splunk skills don't fully transfer to Elastic or Sentinel
Splunk licensing costs make personal lab environments expensive

prerequisites.experience: Linux system administration experience strongly recommended. SIEM engineering is fundamentally infrastructure engineering with security data.

cert_sequence.notes: Splunk Core Certified Power User — SPL query proficiency foundation. Required before Admin cert. Splunk offers a free developer license for lab practice.
Splunk Enterprise Certified Admin — deployment architecture, forwarder management, indexes, data inputs, user management. Core platform engineering credential.
Splunk Certified Consultant — advanced deployment patterns, distributed search, data fabric, and enterprise-scale architecture. Senior platform engineering credential.

paths.name: Microsoft Sentinel Track
paths.rationale: For SIEM engineers whose primary platform is Microsoft Sentinel — the fastest-growing cloud-native SIEM.
pros: SC-200 widely required at Microsoft-focused organizations
Low cost relative to Splunk certification path
Growing deployment base as organizations migrate to cloud SIEM

prerequisites.experience: Azure or Microsoft 365 administration experience beneficial.

cert_sequence.notes: Microsoft Security Operations Analyst — Sentinel operations, ingestion configuration, analytics rule management, and investigation workflows. Foundation for Sentinel engineering.
Microsoft Cybersecurity Architect — adds the architectural layer covering Sentinel workspace design and integration patterns. Opens senior Sentinel engineering and architecture roles.
